transcript: police. Six weeks ago, fire destroyed a paper shredding facility up in Ogden and threatened the jobs of the people with disabilities who work there. Tonight, as news specialist Jed Boll reports, the community has rallied around Enable Utah and learned more about its workers in the process. Jed? Right now, this work crew is in the process of shredding as many as 12,000 pounds of paper documents all in one day. Six weeks ago, when Enable Utah's shredding facility went up in flames, such a quick turnaround did not seem possible. But as you can see, the community stepped up to help save the jobs and the work. Enable Utah provides job training and work for people with cognitive and physical disabilities. Our goal is just to get everyone working that wants to work. And ultimately, get a job in the community. I was really, really shocked. Really depressed. Abby Nelson, a 10-year employee, was at work that day. I just couldn't believe it was happening. Workers feared they would lose their paychecks. It means everything to me. It keeps my independence. Josh Nelson says his job here keeps him calm and happy. It keeps me focused. It keeps me to certain things where I'm not depressed or anything else like that. Ogden businesses offer donations and services. One of them specifically made a donation and said here's some money that we'd like to donate to you so that you can keep paying the individuals with disabilities the disabled employees their same rate of pay enable Utah took out loans and bought shredding equipment to run in the company's main building the tragedy in my mind is not that we had a fire as much as the impact that it could have had on these disabled employees' lives. Business owners who had not even heard of Enable Utah are now interested. I would like to talk to you about trying to employ some of your disabled employees. Enable Utah is holding a couple of fundraising concerts May 4th and May 5th with the Bar J. Wranglers at 7 p.m. in order to help pay for those new shredders. For more information, check out their website, enableutah.org. For KSL 5 News, I'm Jed Boll. Great follow-up, Jed. Thanks.
